name: Jizzakh
description: Jizzakh, regional capital since 1973 on the Sangzor River between Tashkent and Samarkand at the northern foot of the Nuratau range, has 188,000+ people (2023) with industry and agriculture. Ancient Silk Road city known for the Ilonoti (Amir Temur) gorge and the scenic Zomin mountains.

monuments.title: Amir Temur Gate (Ilonoti Gorge)
monuments.info: Strategic Sangzor gorge linking Nuratau and Mogoltau — historic military and trade route; cliffs and inscriptions linked to the Timurid era.

monuments.title: Orda Ecological Park
monuments.info: Green culture and recreation park in the city centre on the site of an old fortress — heritage and nature combined.

monuments.title: Pishagor Cave
monuments.info: Four natural caves in Peshagor gorge, Forish District; 8th–12th c. Khoja Sirab Ota shrine and Niyatlar spring; stalactites and prehistoric finds.

monuments.title: Khojamushkent Ota shrine
monuments.info: Medieval sacred site in Gallaorol District among arch groves and springs — healing water and landscape.

monuments.title: Zomin National Park (Switzerland of Uzbekistan)
monuments.info: Nature reserve at 2000–2500 m: juniper forests, clean air, “Sherafkan” fortress ruins and ancient arch — key ecotourism area.
